sections.paragraph:
Original: ידיתוּן ידתוּן ידוּתוּן

Transliteration: yedûthûn yedûthûn yedı̂ythûn

Phonetic: yed-oo-thoon'

BDB Definition:

Jeduthun = "praising"
  1. a Levite of the family of Merari and the chief of one of the 3 choirs of the temple whose descendants also formed one of the perpetual temple choirs; he is named at the beginning of 3 Psalms (Psa 39:1-13, Psa 62:1-12, 77) either as the composer or the recipient of those Psalms but most likely as the leader of the choir by which those Psalms were to be sung

Origin: probably from H3034

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Masculine
